step_by_step_thinking:
The key piece of information here relates to reducing the chance of Febrile Non-Hemolytic Transfusion Reactions (FNHTRs). FNHTRs occur due to leukocytes present in donor blood triggering an immune response in recipients. Therefore, removing leukocytes through leucocyte-reduction significantly reduces the risk of FNHTRs. None of the other options mentioned (irradiation, washing, glycolisation) specifically address the removal of leukocytes, making C (Leucocyte Reduction) the best option.
\boxed{C} Based on the evidence provided, irradiation, washing, and glycolisation do not target the primary cause of FNHTRs, which is the presence of leukocytes in donated blood. Only leucocyte reduction effectively removes these problematic cells, thus reducing the likelihood of FNHTRs.
